# Tene

> Agentic Secret Runtime -- local-first encrypted secret management that AI agents understand.

Tene encrypts secrets locally with XChaCha20-Poly1305 + Argon2id and generates AI editor rules (CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules, etc.) so agents can discover and use secrets without ever seeing plaintext values.

## Key Features

- Local-first: secrets never leave your machine unencrypted
- AI-native: auto-generates CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules, .windsurfrules, AGENTS.md so AI agents discover secrets without seeing values
- Runtime injection: `tene run -- CMD` injects secrets as env vars so plaintext never touches disk
- Multi-environment: dev, staging, prod with isolated vaults
- Recovery: BIP-39 12-word mnemonic for master key recovery
- Cross-platform: macOS, Linux, Windows (amd64/arm64)
- Free and open source: MIT license, no limits, no signup

## Quick Start

  curl -fsSL https://tene.sh/install.sh | sh
  tene init              # Create vault + master password + recovery key + AI editor rules
  tene set API_KEY sk_xxx  # Encrypt and store a secret
  tene run -- npm start  # Inject all secrets as env vars and run command

## For AI Agents

Tene auto-generates CLAUDE.md for project-specific context:
  tene init          # Creates .tene/vault.db + CLAUDE.md with secret references

Key commands for agents:
  tene list          # List available secret keys (values masked)
  tene run -- CMD    # Inject secrets into any command as environment variables
  tene env           # Switch between environments (dev/staging/prod)

IMPORTANT: Never use `tene get <KEY>` in AI conversations -- plaintext values may leak into logs.
Use `tene list` to verify secrets exist, and `tene run --` to inject them.

## AI Editor Rules

`tene init` generates context files for multiple AI editors:
  CLAUDE.md          # Claude Code (Anthropic)
  .cursor/rules      # Cursor
  .windsurfrules     # Windsurf (Codeium)
  AGENTS.md          # Codex (OpenAI)
  .gemini/rules      # Gemini

These files teach AI agents to use `tene list` and `tene run` instead of reading .env files.

## Encryption Architecture

Master Password -> Argon2id (64MB, 3 iter) -> Master Key (256-bit)
Master Key -> HKDF-SHA256 -> Encryption Key
Secrets encrypted with XChaCha20-Poly1305 (192-bit nonce, key name as AAD)

## Tech Stack

- CLI: Go 1.25+, Cobra, modernc.org/sqlite
- Landing: Next.js 15, Tailwind CSS v4

## Links

- Website: https://tene.sh
- GitHub: https://github.com/tomo-kay/tene
- License: MIT
